attributes.prompt: how much this reduces the time before a new user gets a meaningful successful run
how strongly this prevents users from applying cardinal ranking where it is the wrong tool
how cheap this is to maintain as the code and models evolve

description: Rank onboarding improvements for an engineer trying cardinal-harness from a fresh clone.

items.text: A five-minute quickstart with one request JSON, one command, one expected top-k output, and one short explanation of the receipt fields.
A guided walkthrough from messy shortlist to attributes, pairwise prompts, cache reuse, report reading, and decision caveats.
A prominent section showing cases where scalar metrics, deterministic sorting, or whole-list ranking are cheaper and more appropriate.
A minimal library example that constructs a MultiRerankRequest, installs a model policy, runs one attribute, and emits a report.
A troubleshooting guide for cache misses, model refusals, cost estimates, non-convergence, and inconsistent pairwise judgments.

name: first_user_path
